entries.title: Initial release
entries.problem: Service designers needed a way to generate working GOV.UK prototypes quickly, without writing code. Existing tools required developer knowledge or produced output that did not meet GDS standards.
entries.approach: Built a web app that takes a plain English service brief and generates a complete GOV.UK Prototype Kit v13 project. The prototype is pushed to GitHub and deployed automatically to a live URL via Render. No terminal, no code, no configuration required.
whatChanged: Start page with service description and what the user needs
3 to 5 question pages with server-side validation
Check your answers page with change links
Confirmation page with generated reference number
At least one ineligibility route
entries.nextSteps: Testing a content review pass (beta) that applies GDS content design rules to question wording, hints, and error messages automatically.

entries.title: Content review pass added (experimental)
entries.problem: Generated prototypes had inconsistent question wording, vague error messages, and hints that did not follow GDS content design standards.
entries.approach: Added an optional second Claude API call after spec generation that rewrites content fields only — question labels, hints, error messages, start page and confirmation content — using a focused set of GDS content design rules. The structural spec is unchanged. If the call fails, generation continues with the original content.
whatChanged: New content-review-prompt.js with GDS content rules
Optional second Claude call in POST /generate-v1
Experimental checkbox on v1 page to enable the feature
Fallback to original spec if content review call fails
entries.nextSteps: A/B test content review on vs off across a range of briefs. If content quality is consistently better, enable by default in a future release.

entries.title: Content quality overhaul
entries.problem: Generated prototypes had inconsistent content quality. Sentences ran too long, error messages were generic, confirmation pages were vague, and ineligibility routes left users at dead ends with no alternative.
entries.approach: Rewrote the v1 generation prompt with specific, enforceable content rules derived from GDS content design principles. Rules cover language, question wording, hint text, error messages, start pages, confirmation pages, ineligibility pages, and sensitive services.
whatChanged: Sentence length capped at 15 words with split rule
Nominalisation ban with specific examples
Error messages must state what went wrong and how to fix it specifically
Confirmation pages must name specific next steps not vague holding statements
Ineligibility pages must offer a concrete alternative not a dead end
Hint text rules: only write if it adds something the label does not
Sensitive service language rules added
Question ordering rule: eligibility first, detail second
entries.nextSteps: Test against 3 briefs and compare output quality to previous version. Apply same rules to gds-prompt-v2.js once confirmed stable.

entries.title: Content quality overhaul — split prompt architecture
entries.problem: A single long prompt caused Claude to deprioritise content rules in favour of structural generation. Content quality was inconsistent — long sentences, generic errors, vague confirmations, dead-end ineligibility pages.
entries.approach: Split responsibilities across two prompts. The main generation prompt handles service design structure only — short and focused. The content review prompt handles all language and content rules using XML-tagged sections, which Claude navigates more reliably than flat bullet lists.
whatChanged: gds-prompt-v1.js reduced to structural and service design rules only
content-review-prompt.js rewritten with XML-tagged rule sections
Language rules: sentence length, nominalisation ban, vocabulary level
Error rules: specific failure matching, blame-free framing
Confirmation rules: named next steps, specific timeframes
Ineligibility rules: concrete alternatives, no dead ends
Sensitive service rules: neutral language, purpose transparency
entries.nextSteps: Test across 3 briefs with content review checkbox enabled. Compare against previous output for sentence length, error specificity, and confirmation quality.
